name: Femasys — OBGYN Office-Based Procedures
description: Independent, office-based OBGYN / women's health practices that perform in-office gynecologic procedures (IUD insertion, contraception counseling, infertility workup, hormone/PCOS management) and are NOT high-acuity fertility centers. The ideal target is a physician-owned general OBGYN practice with an established office-procedure patient flow but no in-house IVF program and no reproductive endocrinologist on staff — those route patients to specialized ART pathways rather than office-based devices. Hospital or health-system affiliation is friction (procurement and budget authority sit elsewhere).
scoring_notes: Office-based gynecologic procedure volume is the qualifier; independence is the wedge. REI on staff and in-house IVF are negative signals — they indicate a specialized fertility center rather than the general-OBGYN office-procedure profile Femasys fits. Hospital/health-system affiliation caps tier at Needs Verification for operator review. bullseye_min_score is high (90) by design: a Bullseye requires strong, broad office-procedure fit, not a single matched service line.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list IUD insertion as a service on their website?
signals.note: Core office-procedure signal — confirms in-office gynecologic procedure flow.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is there a reproductive endocrinologist (REI) listed as a provider on staff at this practice?
signals.note: Negative signal — an REI indicates a specialized fertility center rather than the general-OBGYN office-procedure profile. Reduces fit.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list infertility evaluation or workup as a service they offer?
signals.note: Office-based infertility workup (not IVF) aligns with the in-office diagnostic procedures Femasys supports.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice operate its own in-house IVF or ART (assisted reproductive technology) program?
signals.note: Negative signal — an in-house IVF/ART program routes patients to specialized lab pathways rather than office-based devices. Reduces fit.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list contraception counseling or family planning as a service?
signals.note: Supports the office-based women's-health procedure profile.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list hormone therapy, PCOS management, or hormone-related services?
signals.note: Broadens the office-based women's-health service line.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this appear to be an independent private practice (not hospital-owned, not part of a health system)?
signals.note: THE WEDGE. Independence means direct budget authority and faster device adoption.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is this practice affiliated with, owned by, or part of a hospital or health system?
signals.note: Friction signal — caps tier at Needs Verification for operator review. Procurement and budget authority sit with the parent system, not the practice.
